entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 31 Jul, 2022

entry.content:

  • ‘Operation Blue Sky’: Inside Zip’s fight for survival
  • RBA rate rises tipped to slow bank mortgage growth
  • ‘We asked questions about it’: Small companies warned by regulator of flimsy net-zero claims

  • ‘Whatever you do, make it count’: Kerry O’Brien’s advice to Stan Grant

  • ‘Miracle of a film’: The surprising masterpiece you’ll only see at MIFF

Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

  • Italy shocked by video of fatal attack on African immigrant
  • Signs of US economic slowdown complicate Federal Reserve’s inflation battle
  • Chinese regulator says shadow banking sector needs constant scrutiny to guard against risks spilling over into the economy
  • Another global crash on the horizon as the world’s economic engines sputter
  • Disney+ K-drama Adamas: Ji Sung plays double roles in stylish high-society mystery
  • One of the most exciting literary talents, Chinese-American Meng Jin, burnishes her reputation with short story collection
  • Doctors recommend 2 years of breastfeeding, but other experts stress that whether to do it, and for how long, is always a personal choice
  • Indonesia has been called ‘the world’s most invisible country’, but look beyond Bali and Java and you’ll find a wealth of traditional culture and architecture
  • Dad ‘doused me in kerosene’: Christina Chung on her abusive father, rebellious daughters and her return to modelling at 50
  • ‘It’s not a portrait of me’: Pipilotti Rist, Swiss video artist, on Behind Your Eyelid, her Hong Kong show
  • From the Aztecs to avo, the scoop on ‘avocado’ and ‘guacamole’ – its tropical American origins and how alligator pear entered the mix
  • In war on cancer, new drugs and therapies suggest we are on our way to finding cures
  • 5 stars who made a comeback after retiring: from Cameron Diaz announcing a new Netflix film and Tom Brady returning to football after just 40 days, to Jay-Z being ‘terrible’ at retirement
  • Meet Formula One heiress Tamara Ecclestone, daughter of billionaire Bernie Ecclestone: she’s offering millions for her stolen jewellery – and lives in a mansion near Prince William and Kate Middleton
  • Inside luxury fashion’s sustainability evolution: Gucci, Louis Vuitton and Dior are offering eco-friendly designs, with celebrities from Billie Eilish to Emma Stone voicing their support
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 1 Aug, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Replica of famed Shinden wartime fighter finally graces museum
  • Fresh COVID-19 cases in Tokyo hit 31,541, record for a Sunday
  • Mom charged with murdering son, rearrested over 2nd death
  • Wild monkeys terrorizing Yamaguchi with dozens of attacks
  • Kishida to announce fund to bring visitors to A-bombed cities
  • Companies migrating to Hokkaido amid the pandemic
  • Fujifilm’s Instax printer decorates smartphone photos with AR
  • China says remains of rocket booster fall to Earth
  • Chinese factory activity sinks, weighing on weak economy
  • Pelosi confirms trip to Asia, but no mention of Taiwan
  • Scientists record behemoth in waters 2 km deep off Shizuoka
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

  • Korean and U.S. defense chiefs agree to expand exercises
  • EVs top 1%
  • GS Entec acquiring monopile technology from Sif Netherlands
  • Gasoline prices fall for a fourth straight week in Korea
  • Mayor Oh visits, praises Singapore's public housing projects
  • Tennis wunderkind Cho Se-hyuk does it again — this time in Germany
  • Woo Sang-hyeok takes No. 1 spot on high jump world ranking
  • Lollapalooza 'Magic'
  • Kim Min-jae says he's just a young defender looking to give it his all
  • Yoon's approval rating falls to a record-low 28 percent
  • Hwang Ui-jo appears in Bordeaux's first Ligue 2 game
  • [CELEB] Lim Yoon-a sings, dances, acts and juggles two careers at once
The Korea JoongAng Daily is an English-language daily published by the JoongAng Group, Korea’s leading media group, in association with The New York Times.

entry.content:
